prompt:
You are the SCAFFOLDER (opus) in an autonomous substrate-build loop. You build a generic, reusable Lean substrate module.

## Requirement
# Substrate requirement: ehlich_zeller_mesh

## Goal
The EhlichвҖ“Zeller Chebyshev-Lobatto mesh/norming inequality: a real polynomial of degree вүӨ ОІ is uniformly controlled on [-1,1] by its values on the (k+1) Chebyshev-Lobatto nodes whenever the mesh is oversampled (ОІ < k), with a norming constant depending only on the oversampling ratio.

## Provides (API contract)
- `ehlichZeller_mesh_bound` (or similarly named): for a real polynomial `R` of degree вүӨ `ОІ` and integer `k` with `ОІ < k`,
  `sSup {|R x| : x вҲҲ [-1,1]} вүӨ (1 / Real.cos (ПҖ * ОІ / (2 * k))) * (вЁҶ j вҲҲ Finset.range (k+1), |R (-Real.cos (ПҖ * j / k))|)`.
- A corollary in the "oversampling ratio" form: for `c > 1`, `ОІ вүҘ 1`, integer `k вүҘ cВ·ОІ`, the norming constant `K(c) = 1 / Real.cos (ПҖ / (2*c))` works (uses `ОІ/k вүӨ 1/c` and monotonicity of `sec` on `[0, ПҖ/2)`).

## Statement / milestones
Let `R : в„қ[X]` (or a real polynomial function) with `R.natDegree вүӨ ОІ`, and `k : в„•` with `ОІ < k`. Nodes: `x_j = -cos(ПҖ j / k)`, `j = 0..k` (these are the Chebyshev-Lobatto / extrema points; note x_0 = -1, x_k = 1 are always nodes). Then
    sup_{x вҲҲ [-1,1]} |R(x)| вүӨ sec(ПҖ ОІ / (2k)) В· max_{0 вүӨ j вүӨ k} |R(x_j)|.
Proof route (Bernstein / arccos-speed argument):
1. Put `Q(t) = R(-cos t)`. Then `Q` is a real EVEN trigonometric polynomial of degree вүӨ ОІ on `[0, ПҖ]`, and `sup_{xвҲҲ[-1,1]} |R(x)| = sup_{tвҲҲ[0,ПҖ]} |Q(t)|`.
2. Let `M = sup_{[0,ПҖ]} |Q|`, `S = max_j |Q(ПҖ j / k)|`. WLOG `M > 0`; pick `tвӮҖ` with `Q(tвӮҖ) = M` (replace `Q` by `-Q` if needed). Pick node `j` with `|tвӮҖ вҲ’ ПҖ j / k| вүӨ ПҖ/(2k)`.
3. Bernstein differential inequality for the normalized trig polynomial `U = Q/M` of degree ОІ: `|U'(t)| вүӨ ОІ В· вҲҡ(1 вҲ’ U(t)ВІ)` wherever `|U(t)| < 1`. Hence `d/dt arccos(U(t)) ` has magnitude вүӨ ОІ, so `arccos U` is `ОІ`-Lipschitz.
4. Since `U(tвӮҖ) = 1` (arccos 0), `arccos U(ПҖ j / k) вүӨ ОІ В· |tвӮҖ вҲ’ ПҖ j / k| вүӨ ПҖ ОІ / (2k) < ПҖ/2` (as `ОІ < k`). Monotonicity of cos gives `U(ПҖ j / k) вүҘ cos(ПҖ ОІ / (2k))`, i.e. `S вүҘ |Q(ПҖ j/k)| вүҘ M cos(ПҖ ОІ/(2k))`, so `M вүӨ sec(ПҖ ОІ/(2k)) В· S`. вҲҺ

If Bernstein's inequality for trigonometric polynomials is available in Mathlib, use it directly. If not, the cleanest alternative is the Chebyshev-polynomial extremal route (Rakhmanov / CoppersmithвҖ“Rivlin form) вҖ” decide which is more tractable in Mathlib and take it; the deliverable is the mesh bound with a `sec`-type constant, however proved.

## Standard reference
Ehlich, H. & Zeller, K. (1964), "Schwankung von Polynomen zwischen Gitterpunkten", Mathematische Zeitschrift 86, 41вҖ“44. Also standard as a MarcinkiewiczвҖ“Zygmund / polynomial-mesh (norming-set) inequality; see also Rivlin, "The Chebyshev Polynomials" (1974).

## Intended reuse
Discharges the substrate-gate `lem:ehlich-zeller-chebyshev-lobatto-mesh` of thmsmith run `exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax` / `tv_envelope_rollout_design`. It is consumed by `lem:oversampled-chebyshev-lobatto-norming` (which specializes it to k вүҘ cОІ to get a ОІ-uniform constant K(c)) and thereby by the headline `thm:chebyshev-minimax`'s upper bound. Needs the general-polynomial, general-(ОІ,k) statement (NOT gerrymandered to rollout objects) so it is reusable and Mathlib-shaped.

## May assume / must derive
- MAY assume standard Mathlib real-analysis / trigonometry (cos monotonicity, arccos, `Real.cos`, derivatives, `Polynomial.natDegree`, Chebyshev polynomials `Polynomial.Chebyshev.T`).
- MUST derive: the mesh/norming inequality itself and the `sec(ПҖ/(2c))` corollary. Bernstein's trig inequality: if present in Mathlib, cite/use it; if ABSENT, either prove the needed special case honestly or switch to the Chebyshev-extremal route вҖ” do NOT assume it as an unproven axiom/`sorry`.

## Non-goals (optional)
Sharpness of the constant is not required (any finite `sec`-type / oversampling-ratio constant suffices). Complex polynomials, weighted meshes, and the general MarcinkiewiczвҖ“Zygmund `L^p` theory are out of scope.

## Known building blocks (optional)
- `Polynomial.Chebyshev.T` and its trig identity `T_n (cos Оё) = cos (n Оё)`.
- Real trigonometry: `Real.cos`, `Real.arccos`, monotonicity/derivative lemmas.
- Search Mathlib for existing Bernstein/Markov polynomial inequalities and any norming-set / mesh results before building from scratch.

## Target module (optional)
`Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.EhlichZellerMesh`


## Workspace
- Write/extend Lean files under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/EhlichZellerMesh
- Module prefix (one file per module): CausalSmith.Substrate.EhlichZellerMesh.<Name>
- This is build round 1 of at most 10.

## Your task this turn
0. ASSESS CURRENT STATUS FROM GROUND TRUTH. You are a fresh invocation with NO memory of prior turns вҖ” your only context is this prompt plus what you read from disk. Read the current files under the Lean dir and check their real state (remaining `sorry`s and any errors) via lean-lsp diagnostics or a targeted build. Trust the files over the prior round report (a report can lag a partial filler edit). The report and your prior plan below tell you the INTENT; the files tell you the TRUTH.
1. Search the existing library FIRST (run `npm run search -- "<concept>"` in CausalSmith/tools; use lean-lsp local/leansearch) to reuse, not reinvent.
1b. FETCH THE PRIMARY SOURCE when possible. If the Requirement names a reference (or you find the canonical one), fetch it to ground the exact statement, hypotheses, and naming/conventions you formalize вҖ” via WebFetch/WebSearch or Bash `curl`/`wget`; for an arXiv paper download the LaTeX source and read the `.tex`, not the PDF. Skip only when no source is named or none is reachable; never block on it.
2. UPDATE `plan_markdown` as a STRUCTURED STATUS LEDGER (this IS the memory the next turn inherits вҖ” make it concrete, not a vision doc). Use these sections: `## Done` (decls proven/closed), `## Remaining` (the open sorries, by file), `## Blocked` (what's stuck and why), `## Decisions` (design/API choices + what was tried and rejected, so the next turn doesn't repeat dead ends).
3. WRITE or EXTEND the Lean scaffold: precise statements with `sorry` for unproven proofs. The file(s) MUST compile with only `sorry` warnings (no hard errors). Keep each file focused (<300 lines) with a `/-! -/` module docstring and a docstring on each public declaration (first paragraph = self-contained NL translation).
4. DECIDE how many codex subagents to use to fill the sorries this round, and emit ONE prompt per subagent (partition the sorries as you judge best).

## Integrity (HARD RULES вҖ” non-negotiable)
- Do NOT LAUNDER. Never weaken, narrow, or special-case a statement, and never delete or relax a hypothesis that should be proven, just to make the build pass or satisfy the reviewer. The statements must stay the genuine, general results the Requirement asks for. Strengthening an assumption to dodge a hard case, replacing the real goal with a corollary, or hiding the difficulty in a definition all count as laundering вҖ” do not do it.
- Do NOT create VACUOUS statements. No trivially-true claims, no `True`-like lemmas, no uninhabitable/contradictory hypotheses, no premises that are never satisfiable (an always-false hypothesis makes any conclusion hold for free). Every theorem must have real content and be applicable to the intended inhabited inputs.
- When revising after a failed review, FIX THE PROOF / API вҖ” not the goalposts. Address the findings without diluting what the module proves.
- If a clean, true, non-vacuous statement is genuinely out of reach, choose decision="escalate" with the reason. Escalating is correct; laundering or shipping a vacuous statement is a failure.

## Decisions
- decision="build": you wrote/updated the scaffold and want the fillers to run this round. Provide `codex_prompts` (>=1).
- decision="review": choose this ONLY after you have VERIFIED from ground truth that the module compiles with ZERO remaining sorries and zero errors (confirm via diagnostics/build this turn вҖ” do not infer it from the report) AND the statements are the genuine, non-vacuous results (no laundering). `codex_prompts` may be empty.
- decision="escalate": the goal is wrong, unrealistic, or only reachable by weakening it into something vacuous; set `escalation.reason`. The run halts.

Emit ONLY the StructuredOutput JSON object: { decision, plan_markdown, codex_prompts: [{id, target_decls, prompt}], escalation? }.
`plan_markdown` is your living status ledger (persisted to plan.md and fed back to you next turn) вҖ” keep it accurate.

{"decision":"build","plan_markdown":"## ehlich_zeller_mesh вҖ” build ledger (round 1)

### Layout

Definitions in scope: `czTrig R t = R.eval (-Real.cos t)`; `czSup R = sSup ((fun t => |czTrig R t|) '' Set.Icc 0 Real.pi)`.

Proof guidance per lemma (EASY ones first; the crux `czTrig_szego_deriv` is genuinely hard вҖ” attempt it seriously but if truly stuck leave ONLY that one `sorry` and close the other six):

1. `czTrig_continuous`: `czTrig R = fun t => R.eval (-Real.cos t)`. Use `Polynomial.continuous R` composed with `(continuous_neg.comp Real.continuous_cos)`; `unfold czTrig` then `fun_prop` or explicit `.comp`.

2. `czSup_nonneg`: `czSup R` is an sSup of a set of absolute values. The set is nonempty (e.g. `0 вҲҲ Icc 0 ПҖ` since `Real.pi_nonneg`) and bounded above (continuous image of compact `Icc 0 ПҖ` вҖ” see lemma 3's tools). Use `Real.sSup_nonneg`: every element `вүҘ 0` (they are `|В·|`). `Real.sSup_nonneg` needs `вҲҖ x вҲҲ S, 0 вүӨ x`.

3. `czSup_attained`: `|czTrig R|` is continuous (from lemma 1, `.abs`) on the compact nonempty `Set.Icc 0 Real.pi` (`isCompact_Icc`, nonempty via `Real.pi_nonneg`). Use `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` / `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq` to get `tвӮҖ` with `|czTrig R tвӮҖ| = sSup (image) = czSup R`. Search: `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq_and_ge` or `IsCompact.exists_forall_ge`. `czSup R` is exactly `sSup (image)` by definition, so `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq` is the cleanest.

4. `abs_czTrig_le_czSup`: `|czTrig R t| вүӨ sSup (image)` for `t вҲҲ Icc 0 ПҖ` via `le_csSup`, needing `BddAbove (image)` (continuous image of compact `Icc` is bounded: `IsCompact.bddAbove_image` with `(czTrig_continuous R).abs.continuousOn` and `isCompact_Icc`) and membership `вҹЁt, ht, rflвҹ©`.

5. `czTrig_szego_deriv` (DEEP CRUX вҖ” SzegЕ‘'s inequality `|deriv (czTrig R) t| вүӨ ОІВ·вҲҡ(MВІвҲ’(czTrig R t)ВІ)`, `M=czSup R`): This is Bernstein/SzegЕ‘ for trigonometric polynomials; it is NOT in Mathlib. `Q(t)=czTrig R t = R(-cos t)` is a real trig polynomial of degree вүӨ `natDegree R вүӨ ОІ`. Standard proof (Riesz): expand `Q(t)=ОЈ_{n=0}^{ОІ} a_n cos(n t)` (write `R` in the Chebyshev basis `Polynomial.Chebyshev.T`, use `T_real_cos : eval (cos Оё) (T в„қ n) = cos (n Оё)`, and `-cos t = cos(ПҖвҲ’t)`), then apply the Riesz interpolation formula `Q'(t) = (1/(2ОІ)) ОЈ_{k} (-1)^{k+1} / sinВІ(Оё_k/2) В· Q(t+Оё_k)` with `Оё_k=(2kвҲ’1)ПҖ/(2ОІ)`, giving `|Q'(t)| вүӨ ОІВ·max|Q| = ОІВ·M`, and the sharper SzegЕ‘ form `Q'(t)ВІ+ОІВІQ(t)ВІ вүӨ ОІВІMВІ`. This is a large formalization; if you cannot complete it this round, leave EXACTLY this one `sorry` in place (statement unchanged) and ensure the file still builds with only sorry-warnings.

6. `czTrig_arccos_lipschitz` (ОІ-Lipschitz of `t вҶҰ arccos(Q t/M)` on `[0,ПҖ]`, modulo lemma 5): Let `ПҶ t = Real.arccos (czTrig R t / czSup R)`. Where `|Q|<M`, `deriv ПҶ t = -(Q'/M)/вҲҡ(1-(Q/M)ВІ)`, and `вҲҡ(1-(Q/M)ВІ)=вҲҡ(MВІ-QВІ)/M`, so `|deriv ПҶ t| = |Q'|/вҲҡ(MВІ-QВІ) вүӨ ОІ` by lemma 5. Conclude Lipschitz via the MeanValue boundary lemma `image_norm_le_of_norm_deriv_right_le_deriv_boundary'` (or `Convex.norm_image_sub_le_of_norm_deriv_le_segment` / `LipschitzOnWith` from a derivative bound) applied to `ПҶ` on the segment between `s` and `t`; `ПҶ` is continuous (arccosвҲҳcontinuous) so the boundary points where `Q=ВұM` are handled by continuity. If lemma 5 is still `sorry`, you may still complete this by CITING `czTrig_szego_deriv` as a hypothesis-lemma (it is in scope regardless of whether its own proof is done).

7. `czTrig_maximizer_bound` (modulo lemma 6): `ПҶ tвӮҖ = arccos(M/M)=arccos 1 = 0` (`Real.arccos_one`, using `hmax : czTrig R tвӮҖ = czSup R`). By lemma 6, `ПҶ s = |ПҶ s - ПҶ tвӮҖ| вүӨ ОІ|tвӮҖ-s|`. Also `ПҶ s вүҘ 0` (`Real.arccos_nonneg`). Since `ОІ|tвӮҖ-s| вүӨ ПҖ/2` and `cos` is antitone on `[0,ПҖ]` (`Real.cos_le_cos_of_nonneg_of_le_pi` or `Real.strictAntiOn_cos`), `cos(ПҶ s) вүҘ cos(ОІ|tвӮҖ-s|)`. Finally `cos(ПҶ s) = czTrig R s / M` because `arccos` then `cos` recovers the value when `|czTrig R s / M| вүӨ 1` (`Real.cos_arccos`, with `-1 вүӨ Q s/M вүӨ 1` from `abs_czTrig_le_czSup` at `s` plus `hM`). Multiply through by `M>0`.

{"decision":"build","plan_markdown":"## ehlich_zeller_mesh вҖ” build ledger (round 1)

### Layout
Two files under `CausalSmith/Substrate/EhlichZellerMesh/` (module prefix `CausalSmith.Substrate.EhlichZellerMesh`). Both compile with ONLY `sorry` warnings (verified via `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.EhlichZellerMesh.Mesh`, exit 0, this round).

- `Bernstein.lean` вҖ” trig transform `czTrig R t = R.eval (-cos t)`, its sup-norm `czSup R` over `[0,ПҖ]`, soft-analysis facts, and the analytic crux (SzegЕ‘/Bernstein).
- `Mesh.lean` (imports Bernstein) вҖ” nodes `czNode k j = -cos(ПҖ j/k)`, mesh max `czMeshMax`, headline `ehlichZeller_mesh_bound`, and corollary `oversampled_norming`.

### Statements (FROZEN вҖ” genuine, general, non-vacuous; do NOT weaken)
- `ehlichZeller_mesh_bound R ОІ k (hОІ : natDegree вүӨ ОІ) (hk : ОІ < k)`: `sSup(|R|''Icc(-1)1) вүӨ (1/cos(ПҖВ·ОІ/(2k)))В·czMeshMax R k`.
- `oversampled_norming R ОІ k c (hc:1<c)(hОІ:1вүӨОІ)(hОІR)(hk:cВ·ОІвүӨk)`: same LHS `вүӨ (1/cos(ПҖ/(2c)))В·czMeshMax R k`.
Both quantify over an arbitrary real polynomial and arbitrary `(ОІ,k)` вҖ” NOT specialized to any rollout object.

### Done
- Scaffold compiles; all statements type-check. Nothing proven yet (all bodies `sorry`).
- Library survey: Mathlib has NO Bernstein/SzegЕ‘ trig inequality and no polynomial-mesh/norming result (checked leansearch/loogle/local). Available: `Polynomial.Chebyshev.T_real_cos` (`T n (cos Оё)=cos(nВ·Оё)`), full `Real.arccos` API (`antitone_arccos`, `arccos_le_arccos`, `cos_arccos`, `arccos_cos`, `arccos_one`/`arccos_neg_one`), MeanValue boundary lemmas (`image_norm_le_of_norm_deriv_right_le_deriv_boundary'`).

### Remaining (open sorries)
`Bernstein.lean`: `czTrig_continuous`, `czSup_nonneg`, `czSup_attained`, `abs_czTrig_le_czSup`, `czTrig_szego_deriv` (**deep crux**), `czTrig_arccos_lipschitz`, `czTrig_maximizer_bound`.
`Mesh.lean`: `czTrig_at_meshParam`, `exists_meshParam_close`, `czMeshLHS_le_czSup`, `eval_node_le_czMeshMax`, `ehlichZeller_mesh_bound`, `oversampled_norming`.

### Blocked / risk
- The one genuinely deep sorry is `czTrig_szego_deriv` (SzegЕ‘'s differential inequality for trig polynomials). Not in Mathlib; must be proved from the trig-polynomial structure of `czTrig R` (Riesz interpolation formula route) вҖ” this may not close in one round and could need several rounds or eventual escalation if intractable. Everything else is standard real analysis / bookkeeping.

### Decisions
- Route: Bernstein/arccos-speed argument (per requirement), NOT the Chebyshev-extremal route вҖ” arccos monotonicity + MeanValue boundary lemma is the most Mathlib-shaped path.
- Isolated the deep content into a single lemma `czTrig_szego_deriv`; `czTrig_arccos_lipschitz` and `czTrig_maximizer_bound` are downstream of it and are "easy modulo the crux", so the assembly (`Mesh.lean`) can be completed citing them even if the crux stays sorried.
- Sign WLOG (maximizer with `czTrig tвӮҖ = ВұM`) is pushed to `Mesh.lean` via replacing `R` by `-R`; `czTrig_maximizer_bound` is stated only for the `+M` case.
- RHS kept as the requirement's `вЁҶ j вҲҲ Finset.range (k+1)` form (wrapped in `czMeshMax`) to match the API contract, even though it equals a finite `Finset.max`.

Proof guidance per lemma (EASY ones first; the crux `czTrig_szego_deriv` is genuinely hard вҖ” attempt it seriously but if truly stuck leave ONLY that one `sorry` and close the other six):

1. `czTrig_continuous`: `czTrig R = fun t => R.eval (-Real.cos t)`. Use `Polynomial.continuous R` composed with `(continuous_neg.comp Real.continuous_cos)`; `unfold czTrig` then `fun_prop` or explicit `.comp`.

2. `czSup_nonneg`: `czSup R` is an sSup of a set of absolute values. The set is nonempty (e.g. `0 вҲҲ Icc 0 ПҖ` since `Real.pi_nonneg`) and bounded above (continuous image of compact `Icc 0 ПҖ` вҖ” see lemma 3's tools). Use `Real.sSup_nonneg`: every element `вүҘ 0` (they are `|В·|`). `Real.sSup_nonneg` needs `вҲҖ x вҲҲ S, 0 вүӨ x`.

3. `czSup_attained`: `|czTrig R|` is continuous (from lemma 1, `.abs`) on the compact nonempty `Set.Icc 0 Real.pi` (`isCompact_Icc`, nonempty via `Real.pi_nonneg`). Use `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` / `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq` to get `tвӮҖ` with `|czTrig R tвӮҖ| = sSup (image) = czSup R`. Search: `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq_and_ge` or `IsCompact.exists_forall_ge`. `czSup R` is exactly `sSup (image)` by definition, so `IsCompact.exists_sSup_image_eq` is the cleanest.

4. `abs_czTrig_le_czSup`: `|czTrig R t| вүӨ sSup (image)` for `t вҲҲ Icc 0 ПҖ` via `le_csSup`, needing `BddAbove (image)` (continuous image of compact `Icc` is bounded: `IsCompact.bddAbove_image` with `(czTrig_continuous R).abs.continuousOn` and `isCompact_Icc`) and membership `вҹЁt, ht, rflвҹ©`.

5. `czTrig_szego_deriv` (DEEP CRUX вҖ” SzegЕ‘'s inequality `|deriv (czTrig R) t| вүӨ ОІВ·вҲҡ(MВІвҲ’(czTrig R t)ВІ)`, `M=czSup R`): This is Bernstein/SzegЕ‘ for trigonometric polynomials; it is NOT in Mathlib. `Q(t)=czTrig R t = R(-cos t)` is a real trig polynomial of degree вүӨ `natDegree R вүӨ ОІ`. Standard proof (Riesz): expand `Q(t)=ОЈ_{n=0}^{ОІ} a_n cos(n t)` (write `R` in the Chebyshev basis `Polynomial.Chebyshev.T`, use `T_real_cos : eval (cos Оё) (T в„қ n) = cos (n Оё)`, and `-cos t = cos(ПҖвҲ’t)`), then apply the Riesz interpolation formula `Q'(t) = (1/(2ОІ)) ОЈ_{k} (-1)^{k+1} / sinВІ(Оё_k/2) В· Q(t+Оё_k)` with `Оё_k=(2kвҲ’1)ПҖ/(2ОІ)`, giving `|Q'(t)| вүӨ ОІВ·max|Q| = ОІВ·M`, and the sharper SzegЕ‘ form `Q'(t)ВІ+ОІВІQ(t)ВІ вүӨ ОІВІMВІ`. This is a large formalization; if you cannot complete it this round, leave EXACTLY this one `sorry` in place (statement unchanged) and ensure the file still builds with only sorry-warnings.

6. `czTrig_arccos_lipschitz` (ОІ-Lipschitz of `t вҶҰ arccos(Q t/M)` on `[0,ПҖ]`, modulo lemma 5): Let `ПҶ t = Real.arccos (czTrig R t / czSup R)`. Where `|Q|<M`, `deriv ПҶ t = -(Q'/M)/вҲҡ(1-(Q/M)ВІ)`, and `вҲҡ(1-(Q/M)ВІ)=вҲҡ(MВІ-QВІ)/M`, so `|deriv ПҶ t| = |Q'|/вҲҡ(MВІ-QВІ) вүӨ ОІ` by lemma 5. Conclude Lipschitz via the MeanValue boundary lemma `image_norm_le_of_norm_deriv_right_le_deriv_boundary'` (or `Convex.norm_image_sub_le_of_norm_deriv_le_segment` / `LipschitzOnWith` from a derivative bound) applied to `ПҶ` on the segment between `s` and `t`; `ПҶ` is continuous (arccosвҲҳcontinuous) so the boundary points where `Q=ВұM` are handled by continuity. If lemma 5 is still `sorry`, you may still complete this by CITING `czTrig_szego_deriv` as a hypothesis-lemma (it is in scope regardless of whether its own proof is done).

Since `ОІ|tвӮҖ-s| вүӨ ПҖ/2` and `cos` is antitone on `[0,ПҖ]` (`Real.cos_le_cos_of_nonneg_of_le_pi` or `Real.strictAntiOn_cos`), `cos(ПҶ s) вүҘ cos(ОІ|tвӮҖ-s|)`. Multiply through by `M>0`.

   - First `czMeshLHS_le_czSup` reduces the goal to `czSup R вүӨ (1/cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)))В·czMeshMax R k`. Note `cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)) > 0` since `0 вүӨ ПҖ*ОІ/(2k) < ПҖ/2` (from `ОІ<k`, so `ПҖ*ОІ/(2k) < ПҖ/2`); get positivity via `Real.cos_pos_of_mem_Ioo`.
   - If `czSup R = 0`: RHS вүҘ 0 (`czSup_nonneg`, `czMeshMax` вүҘ0, `1/cos>0`), done.
   - Else `czSup R > 0`. Get maximizer `tвӮҖ` from `czSup_attained`: `|czTrig R tвӮҖ| = czSup R`. Two sign cases via `abs_eq` (`czSup_nonneg`): either `czTrig R tвӮҖ = czSup R`, OR `czTrig R tвӮҖ = -czSup R`. In the second case apply the whole argument to `-R` (add private helper lemmas: `czTrig (-R) t = -czTrig R t`, `czSup (-R) = czSup R` [set of `|В·|` unchanged since `|-a|=|a|`], `(-R).natDegree = R.natDegree` via `Polynomial.natDegree_neg`, `(-R).eval x = -(R.eval x)`, and `czMeshMax (-R) k = czMeshMax R k`), reducing to the `+` case; note `czTrig (-R) tвӮҖ = czSup (-R)`.
   - `+` case: from `exists_meshParam_close k (by omega) (mem for tвӮҖ)` get `jвҲҲrange(k+1)` with `|tвӮҖ - ПҖ*j/k| вүӨ ПҖ/(2k)`. Set `s = ПҖ*j/k вҲҲ Icc 0 ПҖ` (since `0 вүӨ j вүӨ k`). Then `ОІ*|tвӮҖ-s| вүӨ ОІ*(ПҖ/(2k)) = ПҖ*ОІ/(2k) вүӨ ПҖ/2` (needs `ОІ вүӨ k`). Apply `czTrig_maximizer_bound` вҮ’ `czSup R * cos(ОІ*|tвӮҖ-s|) вүӨ czTrig R s`. Now `cos(ОІ*|tвӮҖ-s|) вүҘ cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k))` (cos antitone on `[0,ПҖ]`, both args in `[0,ПҖ/2]`, and `ОІ*|tвӮҖ-s| вүӨ ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)`), so `czSup R * cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)) вүӨ czTrig R s вүӨ |czTrig R s| = |R.eval (czNode k j)|` (via `czTrig_at_meshParam`) `вүӨ czMeshMax R k` (`eval_node_le_czMeshMax`). Divide by `cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k))>0`: `czSup R вүӨ (1/cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)))В·czMeshMax R k`. Use `div_le_iff`/`le_div_iff` carefully.

6. `oversampled_norming`: reduce to `ehlichZeller_mesh_bound R ОІ k hОІR (hk':ОІ<k)`. Derive `ОІ<k` from `c*ОІвүӨk`, `c>1`, `ОІвүҘ1` (so `k вүҘ c*ОІ > ОІ`; cast в„•/в„қ carefully, `Nat.cast_lt`). Then it suffices `(1/cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)))В·czMeshMax вүӨ (1/cos(ПҖ/(2c)))В·czMeshMax`, i.e. `1/cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)) вүӨ 1/cos(ПҖ/(2c))` (czMeshMax вүҘ 0). From `c*ОІ вүӨ k` get `ОІ/k вүӨ 1/c` hence `ПҖ*ОІ/(2k) вүӨ ПҖ/(2c) < ПҖ/2`; both cos values positive; `cos` antitone вҮ’ `cos(ПҖ/(2c)) вүӨ cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k))` вҮ’ `1/cos(ПҖ*ОІ/(2k)) вүӨ 1/cos(ПҖ/(2c))` (`one_div_le_one_div_of_le` with positivity). Combine.

Work lemma-by-lemma, rebuild after each with the command above. Keep the existing docstrings. Report which sorries remain."}]}
